```text
1	import { openDB, db_put, db_get, db_delete, db_getAll, getProgress, getRecentlyWatched } from './db.js';
2	import { scanDirectory, generateThumbnail, formatSize } from './scanner.js';
3	import { parseM3U, buildPlaylistFromVideos, sortVideos } from './playlist.js';
4	import { Player } from './player.js';
5	import { initCast, isCastAvailable, castMedia, isLocalUrl } from './cast.js';
6	
7	// ─── State ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
8	
9	const state = {
10	  folders: [],      // { id, name, handle }
11	  videos: [],       // all scanned VideoEntry[]
12	  filtered: [],     // after search/filter
13	  playlist: [],     // current play queue
14	  currentIndex: -1,
15	  player: null,
16	  layout: 'grid',
17	  sort: 'name',
18	  castReady: false,
19	};
20	
21	// ─── Init ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
22	
23	async function init() {
24	  await openDB();
25	  setupLibraryUI();
26	  setupPlayerUI();
27	  setupCast();
28	  await loadSavedFolders();
29	  renderRecent();
30	}
31	
32	// ─── Library UI ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
33	
34	function setupLibraryUI() {
35	  document.getElementById('open-folder-btn').addEventListener('click', pickFolder);
36	  document.getElementById('add-folder-btn').addEventListener('click', pickFolder);
37	  document.getElementById('empty-open-btn').addEventListener('click', pickFolder);
38	
39	  document.getElementById('load-playlist-btn').addEventListener('click', () => {
40	    document.getElementById('playlist-file-input').click();
41	  });
42	  document.getElementById('playlist-file-input').addEventListener('change', onPlaylistFileSelected);
43	
44	  document.getElementById('search-input').addEventListener('input', (e) => {
45	    filterAndRender(e.target.value.trim());
46	  });
47	
48	  document.getElementById('sort-select').addEventListener('change', (e) => {
49	    state.sort = e.target.value;
50	    filterAndRender(document.getElementById('search-input').value.trim());
51	  });
52	
53	  document.querySelectorAll('.view-toggle').forEach(btn => {
54	    btn.addEventListener('click', () => {
55	      document.querySelectorAll('.view-toggle').forEach(b => b.classList.remove('active'));
56	      btn.classList.add('active');
57	      state.layout = btn.dataset.layout;
58	      const grid = document.getElementById('video-grid');
59	      grid.classList.toggle('layout-list', state.layout === 'list');
60	    });
61	  });
62	}
63	
64	// ─── Player UI ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
65	
66	function setupPlayerUI() {
67	  state.player = new Player({
68	    onEnded: () => playNext(),
69	    onBack: () => showLibrary(),
70	  });
71	  state.player.onPrev = () => playPrev();
72	
73	  window.addEventListener('videoError', () => {
74	    const v = state.videos[state.currentIndex];
75	    if (v) toast(`Couldn't play "${v.name}" — format may not be supported by this browser`, 'error');
76	  });
77	}
78	
79	// ─── Cast ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
80	
81	function setupCast() {
82	  initCast((sessionState) => {
83	    if (sessionState === 'available' || sessionState === 'SESSION_STARTED') {
84	      state.castReady = true;
85	      document.getElementById('cast-btn-player').hidden = false;
86	    }
87	  });
88	
89	  document.getElementById('cast-btn-player').addEventListener('click', async () => {
90	    const v = state.playlist[state.currentIndex];
91	    if (!v) return;
92	
93	    const url = state.player.getCurrentUrl();
94	    if (isLocalUrl(url)) {
95	      toast('Chromecast cannot stream local files directly.\nFor local casting, run a media server (Plex, Jellyfin) or access your NAS via HTTP.', 'error');
96	      return;
97	    }
98	    const ok = await castMedia(url, v.title || v.name);
99	    if (!ok) toast('Failed to cast. Make sure a Chromecast device is on the same network.');
100	  });
101	}
102	
103	// ─── Folder management ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
104	
105	async function pickFolder() {
106	  try {
107	    const handle = await window.showDirectoryPicker({ mode: 'read' });
108	    const id = Date.now();
109	    const folder = { id, name: handle.name, handle };
110	    await db_put('folders', { id, name: handle.name });
111	
112	    state.folders.push(folder);
113	    renderFolderList();
114	    await scanFolder(folder);
115	  } catch (err) {
116	    if (err.name !== 'AbortError') toast('Could not open folder.', 'error');
117	  }
118	}
119	
120	async function loadSavedFolders() {
121	  const saved = await db_getAll('folders');
122	  for (const row of saved) {
123	    // We can't restore handles across sessions via plain IndexedDB without serialization —
124	    // show a "Reconnect" entry instead.
125	    const folder = { id: row.id, name: row.name, handle: null, needsReconnect: true };
126	    state.folders.push(folder);
127	  }
128	  renderFolderList();
129	}
130	
131	async function reconnectFolder(folder) {
132	  try {
133	    const handle = await window.showDirectoryPicker({ mode: 'read' });
134	    folder.handle = handle;
135	    folder.name = handle.name;
136	    folder.needsReconnect = false;
137	    await db_put('folders', { id: folder.id, name: folder.name });
138	    renderFolderList();
139	    await scanFolder(folder);
140	  } catch (err) {
141	    if (err.name !== 'AbortError') toast('Could not reconnect folder.', 'error');
142	  }
143	}
144	
145	async function removeFolder(folder, e) {
146	  e.stopPropagation();
147	  await db_delete('folders', folder.id);
148	  state.folders = state.folders.filter(f => f.id !== folder.id);
149	  state.videos = state.videos.filter(v => v.rootName !== folder.name);
150	  renderFolderList();
151	  filterAndRender(document.getElementById('search-input').value.trim());
152	}
153	
154	async function scanFolder(folder) {
155	  showScanning(true);
156	  try {
157	    const found = await scanDirectory(folder.handle, folder.name);
158	    state.videos = state.videos.filter(v => v.rootName !== folder.name);
159	    state.videos.push(...found);
160	    filterAndRender(document.getElementById('search-input').value.trim());
161	    toast(`Found ${found.length} video${found.length !== 1 ? 's' : ''} in "${folder.name}"`);
162	  } catch (err) {
163	    toast('Error scanning folder: ' + err.message, 'error');
164	  } finally {
165	    showScanning(false);
166	  }
167	}
168	
169	function showScanning(on) {
170	  let el = document.getElementById('scanning-indicator');
171	  if (on && !el) {
172	    el = document.createElement('div');
173	    el.id = 'scanning-indicator';
174	    el.className = 'scanning-state';
175	    el.innerHTML = '<div class="spinner"></div><p>Scanning folder…</p>';
176	    document.querySelector('.library-main')?.appendChild(el);
177	    document.getElementById('empty-state').hidden = true;
178	    document.getElementById('video-grid').hidden = true;
179	    document.getElementById('library-toolbar').hidden = true;
180	  } else if (!on && el) {
181	    el.remove();
182	  }
183	}
184	
185	// ─── Rendering ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
186	
187	function renderFolderList() {
188	  const ul = document.getElementById('folder-list');
189	  ul.innerHTML = '';
190	  for (const folder of state.folders) {
191	    const li = document.createElement('li');
192	    li.innerHTML = `
193	      <svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none"><path d="M22 19C22 20.1 21.1 21 20 21H4C2.9 21 2 20.1 2 19V5C2 3.9 2.9 3 4 3H9L11 5H20C21.1 5 22 5.9 22 7V19Z" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2"/></svg>
194	      <span>${folder.name}${folder.needsReconnect ? ' ↩' : ''}</span>
195	      <span class="folder-remove" title="Remove">✕</span>
196	    `;
197	    li.querySelector('.folder-remove').addEventListener('click', (e) => removeFolder(folder, e));
198	    if (folder.needsReconnect) {
199	      li.title = 'Click to reconnect this folder';
200	      li.addEventListener('click', () => reconnectFolder(folder));
201	    } else {
202	      li.addEventListener('click', () => scanFolder(folder));
203	    }
204	    ul.appendChild(li);
205	  }
206	}
207	
208	async function renderRecent() {
209	  const recent = await getRecentlyWatched(10);
210	  const ul = document.getElementById('recent-list');
211	  ul.innerHTML = '';
212	  for (const prog of recent) {
213	    const parts = prog.key.split('::');
214	    const name = parts[parts.length - 1]?.split('/').pop()?.replace(/\.[^.]+$/, '') || prog.key;
215	    const li = document.createElement('li');
216	    li.innerHTML = `<span>${name}</span>`;
217	    li.title = name;
218	    li.addEventListener('click', () => {
219	      const video = state.videos.find(v => v.key === prog.key);
220	      if (video) openPlayer(state.videos.indexOf(video), state.videos);
221	      else toast('Video not found — reopen its folder first.');
222	    });
223	    ul.appendChild(li);
224	  }
225	}
226	
227	function filterAndRender(query = '') {
228	  let list = [...state.videos];
229	  if (query) {
230	    const q = query.toLowerCase();
231	    list = list.filter(v => v.name.toLowerCase().includes(q) || v.relPath.toLowerCase().includes(q));
232	  }
233	  list = sortVideos(list, state.sort);
234	  state.filtered = list;
235	  renderVideoGrid(list);
236	}
237	
238	async function renderVideoGrid(videos) {
239	  const grid = document.getElementById('video-grid');
240	  const empty = document.getElementById('empty-state');
241	  const toolbar = document.getElementById('library-toolbar');
242	
243	  if (videos.length === 0) {
244	    grid.hidden = true;
245	    toolbar.hidden = true;
246	    if (state.videos.length === 0) empty.hidden = false;
247	    return;
248	  }
249	
250	  empty.hidden = true;
251	  toolbar.hidden = false;
252	  document.getElementById('video-count').textContent = `${videos.length} video${videos.length !== 1 ? 's' : ''}`;
253	
254	  grid.hidden = false;
255	  grid.className = `video-grid${state.layout === 'list' ? ' layout-list' : ''}`;
256	  grid.innerHTML = '';
257	
258	  const progressMap = {};
259	  const progs = await db_getAll('progress');
260	  for (const p of progs) progressMap[p.key] = p;
261	
262	  for (const video of videos) {
263	    const prog = progressMap[video.key];
264	    const pct = prog && prog.duration ? Math.round((prog.position / prog.duration) * 100) : 0;
265	    const card = buildCard(video, pct, prog?.completed);
266	    card.addEventListener('click', () => openPlayer(state.filtered.indexOf(video), state.filtered));
267	    grid.appendChild(card);
268	  }
269	}
270	
271	function buildCard(video, pct = 0, completed = false) {
272	  const card = document.createElement('div');
273	  card.className = 'video-card';
274	  card.dataset.key = video.key;
275	
276	  const sizeStr = formatSize(video.size);
277	  const hasSubs = video.subtitles.length > 0;
278	
279	  card.innerHTML = `
280	    <div class="card-thumb">
281	      <div class="card-thumb-placeholder">
282	        <svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none"><path d="M5 3L19 12L5 21V3Z" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.4"/></svg>
283	      </div>
284	      ${pct > 0 ? `<div class="card-progress"><div class="card-progress-fill" style="width:${pct}%"></div></div>` : ''}
285	    </div>
286	    <div class="card-info">
287	      <div class="card-title" title="${video.name}">${video.baseName}</div>
288	      <div class="card-meta">${sizeStr} · ${video.ext.toUpperCase()}</div>
289	      <div class="card-badges">
290	        ${hasSubs ? '<span class="badge badge-sub">CC</span>' : ''}
291	        ${completed ? '<span class="badge badge-watched">Watched</span>' : ''}
292	      </div>
293	    </div>
294	  `;
295	
296	  // Lazy thumbnail via IntersectionObserver
297	  const thumb = card.querySelector('.card-thumb');
298	  const observer = new IntersectionObserver((entries) => {
299	    if (entries[0].isIntersecting) {
300	      observer.disconnect();
301	      loadCardThumbnail(video, thumb);
302	    }
303	  }, { threshold: 0.1 });
304	  observer.observe(card);
305	
306	  return card;
307	}
308	
309	async function loadCardThumbnail(video, thumbEl) {
310	  try {
311	    const cached = await db_get('thumbnails', video.key);
312	    if (cached?.dataUrl) {
313	      setCardThumb(thumbEl, cached.dataUrl);
314	      return;
315	    }
316	  } catch {}
317	
318	  const dataUrl = await generateThumbnail(video.handle);
319	  if (dataUrl) {
320	    setCardThumb(thumbEl, dataUrl);
321	    try {
322	      await db_put('thumbnails', { key: video.key, dataUrl });
323	    } catch {}
324	  }
325	}
326	
327	function setCardThumb(thumbEl, dataUrl) {
328	  const placeholder = thumbEl.querySelector('.card-thumb-placeholder');
329	  if (!placeholder) return;
330	  const img = document.createElement('img');
331	  img.src = dataUrl;
332	  img.alt = '';
333	  img.loading = 'lazy';
334	  placeholder.replaceWith(img);
335	}
336	
337	// ─── Playback ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
338	
339	function openPlayer(index, videoList) {
340	  state.playlist = buildPlaylistFromVideos(videoList);
341	  state.currentIndex = index;
342	
343	  showPlayer();
344	  playAtIndex(index);
345	}
346	
347	function playAtIndex(index) {
348	  if (index < 0 || index >= state.playlist.length) return;
349	  state.currentIndex = index;
350	
351	  const entry = state.playlist[index].video;
352	  state.player.load(entry, true);
353	  renderPlaylistSidebar();
354	  renderRecent();
355	}
356	
357	function playNext() {
358	  if (state.currentIndex < state.playlist.length - 1) {
359	    playAtIndex(state.currentIndex + 1);
360	  } else {
361	    toast('End of playlist');
362	  }
363	}
364	
365	function playPrev() {
366	  if (state.player.video.currentTime > 5) {
367	    state.player.seekTo(0);
368	  } else if (state.currentIndex > 0) {
369	    playAtIndex(state.currentIndex - 1);
370	  }
371	}
372	
373	function renderPlaylistSidebar() {
374	  const ul = document.getElementById('playlist-items');
375	  ul.innerHTML = '';
376	  state.playlist.forEach((item, i) => {
377	    const li = document.createElement('li');
378	    li.className = 'playlist-item' + (i === state.currentIndex ? ' active' : '');
379	    li.innerHTML = `
380	      <span class="playlist-item-num">${i + 1}</span>
381	      <div class="playlist-item-info">
382	        <div class="playlist-item-title" title="${item.title}">${item.title}</div>
383	        <div class="playlist-item-meta">${item.video.ext.toUpperCase()} · ${formatSize(item.video.size)}</div>
384	      </div>
385	      <svg class="playlist-item-playing" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="currentColor"><path d="M5 3L19 12L5 21V3Z"/></svg>
386	    `;
387	    li.addEventListener('click', () => playAtIndex(i));
388	    ul.appendChild(li);
389	  });
390	
391	  // Scroll active item into view
392	  const active = ul.querySelector('.active');
393	  active?.scrollIntoView({ block: 'nearest' });
394	}
395	
396	// ─── M3U playlist ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
397	
398	async function onPlaylistFileSelected(e) {
399	  const file = e.target.files[0];
400	  if (!file) return;
401	  const text = await file.text();
402	  const items = parseM3U(text);
403	
404	  if (!items.length) { toast('No entries found in playlist.', 'error'); return; }
405	
406	  // Try to match items to loaded videos by filename
407	  const matched = [];
408	  for (const item of items) {
409	    const name = item.path.split(/[/\\]/).pop();
410	    const video = state.videos.find(v => v.name === name);
411	    if (video) matched.push(video);
412	  }
413	
414	  if (!matched.length) {
415	    toast(`Playlist loaded but no matching videos found. Open the folder containing the videos first.`, 'error');
416	  } else {
417	    toast(`Playlist: ${matched.length} / ${items.length} videos matched`);
418	    openPlayer(0, matched);
419	  }
420	
421	  e.target.value = '';
422	}
423	
424	// ─── View switching ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
425	
426	function showLibrary() {
427	  document.getElementById('library-view').classList.remove('hidden');
428	  document.getElementById('library-view').classList.add('active');
429	  document.getElementById('player-view').classList.add('hidden');
430	  state.player.video.pause();
431	  renderRecent();
432	  filterAndRender(document.getElementById('search-input').value.trim());
433	}
434	
435	function showPlayer() {
436	  document.getElementById('library-view').classList.add('hidden');
437	  document.getElementById('player-view').classList.remove('hidden');
438	}
439	
440	// ─── Toast ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
441	
442	function toast(msg, type = '') {
443	  const el = document.createElement('div');
444	  el.className = `toast${type ? ' toast-' + type : ''}`;
445	  el.textContent = msg;
446	  document.getElementById('toast-container').appendChild(el);
447	  setTimeout(() => {
448	    el.style.animation = 'toast-out 0.3s ease forwards';
449	    setTimeout(() => el.remove(), 300);
450	  }, 3500);
451	}
452	
453	// ─── Bootstrap ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
454	
455	init().catch(console.error);
456	
457
```

---
name: StreamLocal app project
description: Browser-based local video player with Chromecast support, built at /Users/leocaseiro/Sites/chromecast-local-videos

Pure browser app (no build step) served from /Users/leocaseiro/Sites/chromecast-local-videos. Deployable to GitHub Pages.

**Stack:** Vanilla JS with ES modules, no framework, no bundler. CSS custom properties dark theme.

**File structure:**
- index.html — main layout (library + player views)
- css/style.css — dark cinema theme
- js/app.js — main controller, state, view switching
- js/db.js — IndexedDB wrapper (folders, progress, thumbnails, settings)
- js/scanner.js — File System Access API, recursive folder scan, subtitle detection
- js/subtitles.js — SRT→VTT conversion, track management
- js/playlist.js — M3U parsing, sorting
- js/player.js — custom video player with keyboard shortcuts
- js/cast.js — Google Cast SDK integration (Default Media Receiver CC1AD845)

**Key design decisions:**
- Subtitles detected by same filename + .vtt/.srt in same directory
- Thumbnails generated lazily via IntersectionObserver, cached in IndexedDB
- Watch progress saved every 5s to IndexedDB, auto-resume on next open
- Chromecast works for HTTP URLs only — blob: URLs cannot be cast (browser limitation)
- Folder handles NOT persisted across sessions (OPFS limitation) — user must reconnect with a ↩ indicator
- Dev server: python3 -m http.server from project root (port 8765 used during dev)

**Why:** Local video streaming for Mac with NAS support, subtitles, and Chromecast. Videostream clone.

The app is built and running at `http://localhost:8765`. Here's what was created:

**9 files, zero dependencies (except optional Cast SDK):**

```
index.html         — library + player layout
css/style.css      — dark cinema theme
js/app.js          — main controller, state, view switching
js/db.js           — IndexedDB (progress, thumbnails, settings, folders)
js/scanner.js      — File System Access API, folder scan, subtitle detection
js/subtitles.js    — SRT→VTT conversion, track management
js/playlist.js     — M3U parsing, sorting
js/player.js       — custom player with full keyboard shortcuts
js/cast.js         — Google Cast SDK (Default Media Receiver)
```

**Features working:**
- **Folder picker** — open any local folder or NAS mount (macOS Finder mounts both as local paths)
- **Subtitle auto-detection** — finds `video.vtt` / `video.srt` in the same directory automatically; supports external subtitle upload too
- **Watch progress** — saves to IndexedDB, auto-resumes within 90% watched
- **Thumbnails** — generated lazily via IntersectionObserver, cached in IndexedDB
- **Playlist** — entire folder becomes a queue; `.m3u` files load and match against open folders
- **Keyboard shortcuts** — `Space` play/pause, `←/→` ±10s, `↑↓` volume, `F` fullscreen, `M` mute, `C` subtitles, `N` next, `P` prev
- **Chromecast** — appears when Cast SDK detects a device on the network (requires HTTPS in production = GitHub Pages)

**One known limitation:** Chromecast can't fetch `blob:` local file URLs — it shows a helpful error explaining this. For NAS files accessed via HTTP (Plex, Jellyfin, etc.) it would work normally.

**To deploy to GitHub Pages:** just push the repo as-is — no build step needed.

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-09 13:01:46Z)**_

Videostream uses a **Chrome Extension** that runs `chrome.sockets.tcpServer` — the extension becomes an HTTP server that the Chromecast can reach on the local network. For a plain web app, the equivalent is a tiny companion server process.

The approach: browser uploads the video file to `localhost:PORT`, server stores it in a temp file (local = fast, basically disk I/O speed), and returns an HTTP URL that the Chromecast can actually fetch with full range-request support for seeking.

Let me add this companion server and wire it into the cast flow:

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-09 13:02:04Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```javascript
#!/usr/bin/env node
/**
 * StreamLocal companion server
 * Receives video files from the browser app and re-serves them over HTTP
 * so Chromecast (on the same LAN) can fetch them.
 *
 * Usage: node server.js [port]
 * Default port: 8642
 */

const http = require('http');
const os   = require('os');
const path = require('path');
const fs   = require('fs');
const crypto = require('crypto');

const PORT = parseInt(process.argv[2] || '8642', 10);
const TEMP = os.tmpdir();

// id -> { tempFile, size, contentType, expires }
const sessions = new Map();

// ─── Helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

function getLocalIP() {
  for (const ifaces of Object.values(os.networkInterfaces())) {
    for (const addr of ifaces) {
      if (addr.family === 'IPv4' && !addr.internal) return addr.address;
    }
  }
  return '127.0.0.1';
}

function sanitizeFilename(name) {
  return path.basename(decodeURIComponent(name)).replace(/[^a-zA-Z0-9._-]/g, '_');
}

function addCors(res) {
  res.setHeader('Access-Control-Allow-Origin', '*');
  res.setHeader('Access-Control-Allow-Methods', 'GET, POST, OPTIONS');
  res.setHeader('Access-Control-Allow-Headers', 'Content-Type, X-Filename, Range');
}

function serveWithRanges(filePath, fileSize, contentType, req, res) {
  const range = req.headers['range'];
  if (!range) {
    res.writeHead(200, {
      'Content-Type': contentType,
      'Content-Length': fileSize,
      'Accept-Ranges': 'bytes',
    });
    fs.createReadStream(filePath).pipe(res);
    return;
  }

  const [rawStart, rawEnd] = range.replace(/bytes=/, '').split('-');
  const start = parseInt(rawStart, 10);
  const end   = rawEnd ? parseInt(rawEnd, 10) : fileSize - 1;

  if (start >= fileSize || end >= fileSize || start > end) {
    res.writeHead(416, { 'Content-Range': `bytes */${fileSize}` });
    res.end();
    return;
  }

  res.writeHead(206, {
    'Content-Type': contentType,
    'Content-Length': end - start + 1,
    'Content-Range': `bytes ${start}-${end}/${fileSize}`,
    'Accept-Ranges': 'bytes',
  });
  fs.createReadStream(filePath, { start, end }).pipe(res);
}

function pruneExpired() {
  const now = Date.now();
  for (const [id, entry] of sessions) {
    if (entry.expires < now) {
      try { fs.unlinkSync(entry.tempFile); } catch {}
      sessions.delete(id);
    }
  }
}

// ─── Server ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

const server = http.createServer((req, res) => {
  addCors(res);

  // Preflight
  if (req.method === 'OPTIONS') {
    res.writeHead(204);
    res.end();
    return;
  }

  // Health check – web app pings this to know if server is running
  if (req.url === '/ping') {
    res.writeHead(200, { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' });
    res.end(JSON.stringify({ ok: true, ip: getLocalIP(), port: PORT }));
    return;
  }

  // Upload endpoint – browser POSTs the video file here
  // Headers: X-Filename, Content-Type, Content-Length
  if (req.method === 'POST' && req.url === '/upload') {
    pruneExpired();

    const rawName    = req.headers['x-filename'] || 'video.mp4';
    const filename   = sanitizeFilename(rawName);
    const contentType = req.headers['content-type'] || 'video/mp4';
    const id         = crypto.randomBytes(8).toString('hex');
    const tempFile   = path.join(TEMP, `streamlocal-${id}-${filename}`);

    const writeStream = fs.createWriteStream(tempFile);

    req.on('error', (err) => {
      console.error('Upload error:', err.message);
      try { fs.unlinkSync(tempFile); } catch {}
    });

    req.pipe(writeStream);

    writeStream.on('finish', () => {
      let size = 0;
      try { size = fs.statSync(tempFile).size; } catch {}

      sessions.set(id, {
        tempFile,
        size,
        contentType,
        expires: Date.now() + 2 * 60 * 60 * 1000, // 2h TTL
      });

      const localIP = getLocalIP();
      const castUrl = `http://${localIP}:${PORT}/stream/${id}/${encodeURIComponent(filename)}`;

      console.log(`[cast] ready: ${filename} (${(size / 1024 / 1024).toFixed(1)} MB) → ${castUrl}`);

      res.writeHead(200, { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' });
      res.end(JSON.stringify({ castUrl, id }));
    });

    writeStream.on('error', (err) => {
      console.error('Write error:', err.message);
      res.writeHead(500);
      res.end(JSON.stringify({ error: err.message }));
    });

    return;
  }

  // Stream endpoint – Chromecast fetches video from here
  const match = req.url.match(/^\/stream\/([0-9a-f]+)\//);
  if (req.method === 'GET' && match) {
    const entry = sessions.get(match[1]);
    if (!entry) {
      res.writeHead(404);
      res.end('Not found or expired');
      return;
    }

    let fileSize = entry.size;
    // Refresh size in case a previous stat was wrong
    try { fileSize = fs.statSync(entry.tempFile).size; } catch {}

    serveWithRanges(entry.tempFile, fileSize, entry.contentType, req, res);
    return;
  }

  res.writeHead(404);
  res.end('StreamLocal companion server');
});

server.listen(PORT, '0.0.0.0', () => {
  const ip = getLocalIP();
  console.log('\n🎬  StreamLocal companion server');
  console.log(`    Local:   http://localhost:${PORT}`);
  console.log(`    Network: http://${ip}:${PORT}`);
  console.log('\n    Open the StreamLocal web app and cast away.');
  console.log('    Keep this terminal window open while casting.\n');
});

server.on('error', (err) => {
  if (err.code === 'EADDRINUSE') {
    console.error(`Port ${PORT} is already in use. Try: node server.js 8643`);
  } else {
    console.error(err);
  }
  process.exit(1);
});

```

Good. Here's how the complete Chromecast flow now works:

**The `server.js` companion (zero npm deps, pure Node.js builtins):**
1. Browser pings `localhost:8642/ping` → if no response, shows "run `node server.js`"
2. Browser POSTs the video file to `localhost:8642/upload` with progress tracking
3. Server writes it to a temp file, returns `http://[LAN-IP]:8642/stream/[id]/file.mp4`
4. That URL is what gets sent to the Chromecast — a real HTTP URL on the LAN, with full **range-request support** for seeking

**vs Videostream's approach:** they do the same thing but as a Chrome Extension using `chrome.sockets.tcpServer` — the extension *is* the HTTP server, so no separate process needed. Same concept, different container.

**To cast locally:**
```bash
# in the project folder
node server.js
# then open http://localhost:8765 in Chrome
```

The progress toast will show upload % while the video uploads to the temp server. Since it's all local (disk I/O, not real network), even a 4 GB file uploads in seconds.

What were you looking at at `localhost:5557/ui/`? Is that your existing app or something else?

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-09 13:07:18Z)**_

Three fixes landed:

**Cast icon now visible:** The icon shows in both the library header and the player. It has three visual states:
- Greyed out (35% opacity) — Cast API unavailable (rare on desktop Chrome)
- Normal — ready to cast
- Accent-colored — actively casting

The root bug was a race condition: `type="module"` scripts are deferred, so the Cast SDK was firing `__onGCastApiAvailable` before our module had a chance to register the callback. Fixed by setting up a sync inline listener in `<head>` before the SDK loads.

**Subtitles auto-load:** If a `.vtt` or `.srt` file is found alongside the video, the first track now enables automatically on `loadedmetadata`. Your preference is persisted in IndexedDB — turn subtitles off once via the CC menu and it stays off across videos.

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```javascript
#!/usr/bin/env node
// Static file server for the StreamLocal web app. Zero npm dependencies.
const http = require('http');
const fs   = require('fs');
const path = require('path');

const PORT = parseInt(process.env.PORT || process.argv[2] || '8765', 10);
const ROOT = __dirname;

const MIME = {
  '.html': 'text/html; charset=utf-8',
  '.css':  'text/css; charset=utf-8',
  '.js':   'application/javascript; charset=utf-8',
  '.json': 'application/json; charset=utf-8',
  '.svg':  'image/svg+xml',
  '.ico':  'image/x-icon',
  '.png':  'image/png',
  '.jpg':  'image/jpeg',
  '.webp': 'image/webp',
};

http.createServer((req, res) => {
  let urlPath = req.url.split('?')[0];
  if (urlPath === '/' || urlPath === '') urlPath = '/index.html';

  const filePath = path.join(ROOT, urlPath);

  // Prevent path traversal
  if (!filePath.startsWith(ROOT)) {
    res.writeHead(403); res.end('Forbidden'); return;
  }

  const ext = path.extname(filePath).toLowerCase();
  const contentType = MIME[ext] || 'application/octet-stream';

  fs.readFile(filePath, (err, data) => {
    if (err) {
      res.writeHead(404, { 'Content-Type': 'text/plain' });
      res.end('Not found');
      return;
    }
    res.writeHead(200, { 'Content-Type': contentType, 'Cache-Control': 'no-cache' });
    res.end(data);
  });
}).listen(PORT, '127.0.0.1', () => {
  console.log(`StreamLocal web app → http://localhost:${PORT}`);
});

```

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-09 13:08:08Z)**_

Now you have four commands, all zero-dependency:

```bash
npm start        # web app + cast server together (background processes)
npm run web      # just the web app  → http://localhost:8765
npm run cast     # just the cast server → http://localhost:8642
npm run open     # open the app in your default browser
```

**Typical flow:**
```bash
npm start        # launches both servers
npm run open     # opens http://localhost:8765 in Chrome
```

For casting, `npm start` already runs `server.js` in the background so no extra step. Both processes share the same terminal — `Ctrl+C` stops them both.

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-09 13:23:15Z)**_

Three bugs to fix:

1. **ERR_ALPN_NEGOTIATION_FAILED** — `duplex: 'half'` in fetch forces HTTP/2, which Chrome refuses for plain HTTP. Switch to XHR (also gets real upload progress).
2. **Icon always triangle** — `.hidden = true/false` doesn't work on `SVGElement` (it's an HTML property, not SVG). Need `style.display` instead.
3. **Spurious error toast** — fires when we intentionally clear `src = ''` before loading a new file.

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-09 13:24:25Z)**_

All three bugs fixed:

| Bug | Root cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| Cast "failed to fetch" | `fetch` with `duplex: 'half'` requires HTTP/2; plain HTTP server rejected it | Switched to XHR — also gets real upload progress |
| Icon always triangle | `.hidden` is an `HTMLElement` property; SVGElement ignores it silently | `style.display = 'none'/'` instead |
| Spurious "format not supported" | Clearing `src = ''` between loads fires the error event | Null out `_currentBlobUrl` first so the error handler ignores the intentional clear |

For the cast to work you'll still need to run `node server.js` in a separate terminal (or `npm start` which runs both servers together).

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-09 13:26:55Z)**_

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-09 13:26:57Z)**_

The bug is that we save only `{ id, name }` to IndexedDB — the handle is dropped. But `FileSystemDirectoryHandle` objects **can** be stored in IndexedDB directly (they're structured-cloneable). We can also check permission silently on load with `queryPermission()`, and auto-scan without any picker if the browser already granted access.

_**Agent (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-05-09 13:27:29Z)**_

The fix is in three parts:

1. **Handle now saved to IndexedDB** — `FileSystemDirectoryHandle` is structured-cloneable, so we store it directly: `{ id, name, handle }`. Previous code only saved `{ id, name }`.

2. **Auto-restore on load** — `queryPermission()` doesn't need a user gesture. If Chrome remembers the grant (it usually does for the session and often across restarts), folders re-scan automatically with no prompt.

3. **One-click re-grant** — If permission lapsed (e.g. after a browser restart), the folder shows `↩` in the sidebar. One click calls `requestPermission()` on the *existing* handle — no folder picker, just a browser permission prompt — then scans immediately.

Chrome's permission persistence varies: within the same browser session it's always silent. Across restarts it depends on whether the user has "Ask before accessing" site settings enabled for localhost.

_**User (2026-05-09 13:32:43Z)**_

This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user wanted to build a browser-based local video player called "StreamLocal" — a clone of Videostream (getvideostream.com) — with these features:
   - Browse local folders (including NAS mounts) using the File System Access API
   - Auto-detect subtitles with the same filename as the video (`.vtt` or `.srt` in the same directory)
   - Play videos in Chrome with a custom player UI
   - Chromecast support (cast local files to TV)
   - Load `.m3u` playlists
   - Auto-play / queue management
   - 100% browser-native, deployable to GitHub Pages
   - Persist last-watched position, folder handles, thumbnails in IndexedDB/localStorage

   Over the conversation, additional specific requests were:
   - npm scripts for easy startup
   - Fix "must reopen folder on every reload"
   - Fix Chromecast cast icon not visible
   - Fix play/pause icon not toggling
   - Fix spurious "format not supported" error toast
   - Fix ERR_ALPN_NEGOTIATION_FAILED on cast upload
   - Subtitles should auto-enable (not require manual selection)
   - Implement proper Chromecast sender UI (hide local video when casting, show overlay with device name, wire controls to Remote Player API)
   - Fix Chromecast playlist (only current video plays, next doesn't continue)

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - **File System Access API** (`showDirectoryPicker`, `FileSystemDirectoryHandle`, `queryPermission`, `requestPermission`) — Chrome 86+ only; works on localhost; not supported in Brave/Firefox/Safari
   - **IndexedDB** — stores folder handles (structured-cloneable), watch progress, thumbnail data URLs, settings
   - **Google Cast SDK v3** (`cast.framework.CastContext`, `RemotePlayer`, `RemotePlayerController`, Default Media Receiver `CC1AD845`)
   - **`__onGCastApiAvailable` timing** — must be set synchronously before the Cast SDK script; ES modules are deferred so there's a race condition
   - **`ERR_ALPN_NEGOTIATION_FAILED`** — `fetch` with `duplex: 'half'` requires HTTP/2; plain Node.js HTTP/1.1 rejects it; XHR avoids this
   - **SVGElement vs HTMLElement** — `.hidden` property is only on `HTMLElement`; SVGs need `style.display = 'none'/'`
   - **`[hidden]` CSS override** — custom CSS `display` values override HTML `hidden` attribute; fix with `[hidden] { display: none !important; }`
   - **Chromecast local file problem** — blob: URLs not accessible from Chromecast device; needs a local HTTP server to re-serve files
   - **companion server.js** — pure Node.js (no npm deps), receives video via XHR POST, saves to temp file, returns LAN HTTP URL with range-request support for seeking
   - **WebVTT / SRT conversion** — replace `,ms` with `.ms` timestamps, strip index numbers, add `WEBVTT` header
   - **IntersectionObserver** for lazy thumbnail generation
   - **RemotePlayer / RemotePlayerController** for Cast sender UI (CURRENT WORK)

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - **`index.html`**
     - Main layout: library view (header, sidebar, video grid) + player view (video container, overlay controls, playlist sidebar)
     - Subtitle popup menu, toast container
     - Critical inline script BEFORE Cast SDK: sets `window.__castCallbacks = []` and `window.__onGCastApiAvailable` synchronously
     - Cast overlay HTML added (most recent change):
       ```html
       <div id="cast-overlay" class="cast-overlay" style="display:none">
         <div class="cast-overlay-icon">
           <svg class="cast-icon-main" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none">...</svg>
           <div class="cast-ring cast-ring-1"></div>
           <div class="cast-ring cast-ring-2"></div>
         </div>
         <div class="cast-overlay-label">Casting to <strong id="cast-device-label">your TV</strong></div>
         <div class="cast-overlay-title" id="cast-overlay-title"></div>
       </div>
       ```
     - SVG icons for play/pause, volume, fullscreen now use `style="display:none"` instead of `hidden` attribute

   - **`css/style.css`**
     - Dark cinema theme with CSS custom properties (`--bg-0` through `--bg-5`, `--accent: #7c3aed`)
     - `[hidden] { display: none !important; }` — fixes CSS overriding HTML hidden attribute
     - Cast button states: `[data-cast-state="unavailable"]` = 35% opacity, `[data-cast-state="connected"]` = accent color
     - Cast overlay CSS NOT YET ADDED (pending)

   - **`js/db.js`**
     - IndexedDB wrapper with stores: `folders`, `progress`, `thumbnails`, `settings`
     - Exports: `openDB`, `db_put`, `db_get`, `db_delete`, `db_getAll`, `getSetting`, `setSetting`, `saveProgress`, `getProgress`, `getRecentlyWatched`
     - `folders` store uses `keyPath: 'id'` with autoIncrement

   - **`js/scanner.js`**
     - `scanDirectory(dirHandle, rootName, pathPrefix, depth)` — recursive, max depth 8
     - Subtitle matching: builds `fileMap` of all files in directory, looks for `${baseName}.vtt` and `${baseName}.srt` (case-insensitive via `.toLowerCase()`)
     - `generateThumbnail(fileHandle)` — creates blob URL, seeks video to 5s, draws to canvas, returns JPEG data URL, 8s timeout
     - `VIDEO_EXTS = Set(['mp4', 'webm', 'mkv', 'avi', 'mov', 'wmv', 'm4v', 'ogv', 'flv', 'ts', 'm2ts'])`

   - **`js/subtitles.js`**
     - `srtToVtt(srt)` — strips BOM, normalizes line endings, converts `,ms` → `.ms`, skips numeric index lines
     - `loadSubtitleHandle(handle, videoEl, label)` — reads file, converts if .srt, creates blob URL, calls `addTrack`
     - `addTrack`, `clearTracks`, `disableAllTracks`, `enableTrack`

   - **`js/player.js`**
     - `Player` class with full keyboard shortcuts
     - SVG icon toggling uses `style.display` (NOT `.hidden`) — critical fix:
       ```js
       _onPlay() {
         this.playIcon.style.display = 'none';
         this.pauseIcon.style.display = '';
         this._scheduleHide();
       }
       _onPause() {
         this.playIcon.style.display = '';
         this.pauseIcon.style.display = 'none';
         this._showOverlay(true);
       }
       _updateVolumeUI() {
         const muted = this.video.muted || this.video.volume === 0;
         this.volHigh.style.display = muted ? 'none' : '';
         this.volMuted.style.display = muted ? '' : 'none';
       }
       _onFullscreenChange() {
         const isFs = !!document.fullscreenElement;
         this.fsExpand.style.display = isFs ? 'none' : '';
         this.fsShrink.style.display = isFs ? '' : 'none';
       }
       ```
     - Spurious error fix: nulls `_currentBlobUrl` before `src = ''`, guards error handler:
       ```js
       async load(videoEntry, autoplay = true) {
         if (this._currentBlobUrl) {
           URL.revokeObjectURL(this._currentBlobUrl);
           this._currentBlobUrl = null; // clear before src='' to suppress error
         }
         clearTracks(this.video);
         this.video.src = '';
         ...
       }
       this.video.addEventListener('error', () => {
         if (!this._currentBlobUrl) return; // ignore empty-src clear between loads
         ...
       });
       ```
     - Auto-subtitle loading respects `subtitlesEnabled` setting (default true)
     - Progress saved every 5s to IndexedDB, restored on load (skips if > 90% watched)

   - **`js/cast.js`**
     - `initCast(onStateChange)` — uses `window.__castApiReady` / `window.__castCallbacks` pattern to handle timing
     - `castMedia(videoUrl, title, subtitleUrl)` — uses Default Media Receiver
     - `pingCompanion()` — fetches `http://localhost:8642/ping` with 1.5s timeout
     - `uploadForCast(fileHandle, onProgress)` — uses XHR (not fetch) to POST file:
       ```js
       export async function uploadForCast(fileHandle, onProgress) {
         const file = await fileHandle.getFile();
         return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
           const xhr = new XMLHttpRequest();
           xhr.open('POST', `${COMPANION_URL}/upload`);
           xhr.setRequestHeader('X-Filename', encodeURIComponent(file.name));
           xhr.setRequestHeader('Content-Type', file.type || 'video/mp4');
           xhr.upload.onprogress = (e) => {
             if (e.lengthComputable) onProgress?.(e.loaded / e.total);
           };
           xhr.onload = () => { ... resolve(JSON.parse(xhr.responseText)); };
           xhr.onerror = () => reject(new Error('Cannot reach companion server — is it running? (node server.js)'));
           xhr.send(file);
         });
       }
       ```

   - **`js/app.js`**
     - `init()` — UI setup runs first, storage is best-effort (catches IndexedDB failures for private mode)
     - `pickFolder()` — saves `{ id, name, handle }` to IndexedDB (not just name)
     - `loadSavedFolders()` — restores handles, calls `queryPermission()`, auto-scans if granted, shows `↩` if needs re-prompt
     - `reconnectFolder()` — calls `handle.requestPermission()` on stored handle (no new picker needed)
     - Cast state: `setCastState(state)` updates `data-cast-state` attribute on all `.cast-btn` elements
     - `doCast` handler: pings companion, uploads via XHR, casts URL; shows progress toast
     - `showProgressToast()` — returns `{ update, dismiss }` for long-running operations

   - **`server.js`**
     - Pure Node.js (http, os, path, fs, crypto — no npm deps)
     - `POST /upload` — saves to `os.tmpdir()/streamlocal-{id}-{filename}`, responds immediately with `{ castUrl }`
     - `GET /stream/:id/:filename` — full range-request support for seeking
     - `GET /ping` — health check returning `{ ok, ip, port }`
     - 2-hour TTL on temp files, CORS headers (`Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *`)

   - **`dev-server.js`**
     - Zero-dep static file server, serves from `__dirname`, port 8765
     - MIME type map for html/css/js/json/svg/ico/png/jpg/webp

   - **`package.json`**
     ```json
     {
       "scripts": {
         "start": "node dev-server.js & node server.js",
         "web": "node dev-server.js",
         "cast": "node server.js",
         "open": "open http://localhost:8765"
       }
     }
     ```

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **`[hidden]` CSS override**: `display: grid/flex` in CSS overrides HTML `hidden` attribute. Fixed: `[hidden] { display: none !important; }` added to style.css.
   - **SVGElement `.hidden` property**: Setting `.hidden = true/false` on SVG elements doesn't work (it's an HTMLElement IDL attribute). Fixed: all icon toggles now use `style.display = 'none'/'` and HTML uses `style="display:none"` instead of `hidden` attribute.
   - **Spurious "format not supported" toast**: The `video.addEventListener('error')` fired when `src = ''` was set intentionally between loads. Fixed: null `_currentBlobUrl` before clearing src, guard early-returns in error handler.
   - **ERR_ALPN_NEGOTIATION_FAILED**: `fetch` with `duplex: 'half'` requires HTTP/2. Fixed: switched to XHR for upload.
   - **`__onGCastApiAvailable` race condition**: ES modules deferred → SDK fires callback before module registers it. Fixed: inline `<script>` in `<head>` before SDK sets `window.__castCallbacks = []`.
   - **Folder handles not persisted**: Was saving `{ id, name }` only. Fixed: save `{ id, name, handle }` — FileSystemDirectoryHandle is structured-cloneable and stores fine in IndexedDB.
   - **Two servers on port 8765**: Python + Node both listening. Fixed: killed Python process.
   - **Dynamic imports inside already-imported module**: `loadCardThumbnail` re-imported db.js dynamically. Fixed: uses static imports.
   - **Fragile innerHTML regex replace**: `setCardThumb` used regex. Fixed: `placeholder.replaceWith(img)`.
   - **Brave browser**: No File System Access API. Added `window.showDirectoryPicker` check with informative error.
   - **`queueThumbnails` unused**: Was pushing to `state.thumbnailQueue` but never processing. Removed entirely (IntersectionObserver handles lazy loading).

5. Problem Solving:
   - **Local file Chromecast**: Browser blob: URLs inaccessible from Chromecast. Solution: `server.js` companion — browser XHR-uploads file → server saves to temp → returns LAN HTTP URL → Chromecast fetches it. This mirrors how Videostream's Chrome Extension uses `chrome.sockets.tcpServer`.
   - **Subtitle auto-load**: `getSetting('subtitlesEnabled', true)` checked on each video load; first track enabled automatically on `loadedmetadata`; user preference saved when they toggle via CC menu.
   - **Folder persistence**: `FileSystemDirectoryHandle` stored in IndexedDB. On reload, `queryPermission()` (no gesture needed) checked — if `'granted'`, auto-scan; if `'prompt'`, show `↩` one-click reconnect that calls `requestPermission()` (requires gesture).

```text
83	    window.addEventListener('mouseup', (e) => { if (this._dragging) { this._doSeek(e); this._dragging = false; } });
84	    this.progressBar.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => {

148	  async _restoreVolume() {
149	    const vol = await getSetting('volume', 1);
150	    this.video.volume = vol;
151	    this.volumeSlider.value = vol;
152	    this._updateVolumeUI();
153	  }
154	
155	  async load(videoEntry, autoplay = true) {
156	    if (this._currentBlobUrl) {
157	      URL.revokeObjectURL(this._currentBlobUrl);
158	      this._currentBlobUrl = null; // clear before src='' so error handler ignores it
159	    }
160	    clearTracks(this.video);
161	    this.video.src = '';
162	    this._currentKey = videoEntry.key;
163	
164	    const file = await videoEntry.handle.getFile();
165	    this._currentBlobUrl = URL.createObjectURL(file);
166	    this.video.src = this._currentBlobUrl;
167	    this.titleEl.textContent = videoEntry.baseName;
168	
169	    // Load subtitles: prefer .vtt, fallback to .srt
170	    for (const sub of videoEntry.subtitles) {
171	      const label = sub.ext.toUpperCase();
172	      await loadSubtitleHandle(sub.handle, this.video, label);
173	    }
174	
175	    // Auto-enable first subtitle track if subtitles exist and user hasn't turned them off
176	    if (videoEntry.subtitles.length > 0) {
177	      const subEnabled = await getSetting('subtitlesEnabled', true);
178	      if (subEnabled) {
179	        this.video.addEventListener('loadedmetadata', () => {
180	          const tracks = Array.from(this.video.textTracks);
181	          if (tracks.length > 0 && tracks.every(t => t.mode === 'disabled')) {
182	            tracks[0].mode = 'showing';
183	          }
184	        }, { once: true });
185	      }
186	    }
187	
188	    this._renderSubtitleMenu();
189	
190	    // Restore position
191	    const prog = await getProgress(videoEntry.key);
192	    if (prog && prog.position > 10 && !prog.completed) {
193	      this.video.addEventListener('loadedmetadata', () => {
194	        this.video.currentTime = prog.position;
195	      }, { once: true });
196	    }
197	
198	    this._startProgressSaving();
199	
200	    if (autoplay) {
201	      this.video.play().catch(() => {});
202	    }
203	
204	    this._showOverlay();
205	  }
206	
207	  togglePlay() {
208	    if (this.video.paused) {
209	      this.video.play().catch(() => {});
210	    } else {
211	      this.video.pause();
212	    }
213	    this._flashIndicator(this.video.paused);
214	  }
215	
216	  seek(delta) {
217	    this.video.currentTime = Math.max(0, Math.min(this.video.duration, this.video.currentTime + delta));
218	  }
219	
220	  seekTo(seconds) {
221	    this.video.currentTime = Math.max(0, Math.min(this.video.duration, seconds));
222	  }
223	
224	  toggleMute() {
225	    this.video.muted = !this.video.muted;
226	    if (!this.video.muted && this.video.volume === 0) {
227	      this.video.volume = 0.5;
228	      this.volumeSlider.value = 0.5;
229	    }
230	    this._updateVolumeUI();
231	  }
232	
233	  toggleFullscreen() {
234	    if (!document.fullscreenElement) {
235	      (this.container.requestFullscreen?.() || this.container.webkitRequestFullscreen?.())?.catch(() => {});
236	    } else {
237	      document.exitFullscreen?.() || document.webkitExitFullscreen?.();
238	    }
239	  }
240	
241	  destroy() {
242	    this._stopProgressSaving();
243	    if (this._currentBlobUrl) URL.revokeObjectURL(this._currentBlobUrl);
244	    clearTracks(this.video);
245	    this.video.src = '';
246	    this.video.load();
247	  }
248	
249	  getCurrentKey() { return this._currentKey; }
250	  getCurrentUrl() { return this._currentBlobUrl; }
251	
252	  // ─── Private ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
253	
254	  _onPlay() {
255	    this.playIcon.style.display = 'none';
256	    this.pauseIcon.style.display = '';
257	    this._scheduleHide();
258	  }
259	
260	  _onPause() {
261	    this.playIcon.style.display = '';
262	    this.pauseIcon.style.display = 'none';
263	    this._showOverlay(true);
264	  }
265	
266	  _onVideoEnded() {
267	    this._stopProgressSaving();
268	    this.onEnded();
269	  }
270	
271	  _onTimeUpdate() {
272	    if (this._dragging) return;
273	    const { currentTime, duration } = this.video;
274	    if (!duration) return;
275	    const pct = (currentTime / duration) * 100;
276	    this.progressFill.style.width = `${pct}%`;
277	    this.progressThumb.style.left = `${pct}%`;
278	    this.progressBar.setAttribute('aria-valuenow', Math.round(pct));
279	    this.currentTimeEl.textContent = formatTime(currentTime);
280	  }
281	
282	  _onBufferUpdate() {
283	    const { buffered, duration } = this.video;
284	    if (!duration || !buffered.length) return;
285	    const end = buffered.end(buffered.length - 1);
286	    this.bufferedFill.style.width = `${(end / duration) * 100}%`;
287	  }
288	
289	  _onFullscreenChange() {
290	    const isFs = !!document.fullscreenElement;
291	    this.fsExpand.style.display = isFs ? 'none' : '';
292	    this.fsShrink.style.display = isFs ? '' : 'none';
293	  }
294	
295	  _startSeek(e) {
296	    this._dragging = true;
297	    this._doSeek(e);
298	  }
299	
300	  _doSeek(e) {
301	    const rect = this.progressBar.getBoundingClientRect();
302	    const ratio = Math.max(0, Math.min(1, (e.clientX - rect.left) / rect.width));
303	    const time = ratio * this.video.duration;
304	    if (isFinite(time)) {
305	      this.progressFill.style.width = `${ratio * 100}%`;
306	      this.progressThumb.style.left = `${ratio * 100}%`;
307	      if (!this._dragging || e.type === 'mouseup') {
308	        this.video.currentTime = time;
309	      }
310	    }
311	  }
312	
313	  _onProgressHover(e) {
314	    const rect = this.progressBar.getBoundingClientRect();
315	    const ratio = Math.max(0, Math.min(1, (e.clientX - rect.left) / rect.width));
316	    const time = ratio * (this.video.duration || 0);
317	    this.progressTooltip.textContent = formatTime(time);
318	    this.progressTooltip.style.left = `${ratio * 100}%`;
319	  }
320	
321	  _showOverlay(permanent = false) {
322	    this.overlay.classList.add('visible');
323	    if (!permanent) this._scheduleHide();
324	  }
325	
326	  _scheduleHide() {
327	    clearTimeout(this._hideTimer);
328	    if (this.video.paused) return;
329	    this._hideTimer = setTimeout(() => {
330	      this.overlay.classList.remove('visible');
331	    }, 3000);
332	  }
333	
334	  _updateVolumeUI() {
335	    const muted = this.video.muted || this.video.volume === 0;
336	    this.volHigh.style.display = muted ? 'none' : '';
337	    this.volMuted.style.display = muted ? '' : 'none';
338	    if (!muted) this.volumeSlider.value = this.video.volume;
339	  }
340	
341	  _startProgressSaving() {
342	    this._stopProgressSaving();
343	    this._progressTimer = setInterval(() => {
344	      if (this._currentKey && this.video.duration) {
345	        saveProgress(this._currentKey, this.video.currentTime, this.video.duration);
346	      }
347	    }, PROGRESS_SAVE_INTERVAL);
348	  }
349	
350	  _stopProgressSaving() {
351	    if (this._progressTimer) {
352	      clearInterval(this._progressTimer);
353	      this._progressTimer = null;
354	    }
355	    if (this._currentKey && this.video.duration > 0) {
356	      saveProgress(this._currentKey, this.video.currentTime, this.video.duration);
357	    }
358	  }
359	
360	  _flashIndicator(wasPaused) {
361	    this.playIndicator.textContent = wasPaused ? '⏸' : '▶';
362	    this.playIndicator.classList.remove('flash');
363	    void this.playIndicator.offsetWidth; // reflow
364	    this.playIndicator.classList.add('flash');
365	  }
366	
367	  _toggleSubtitleMenu() {
368	    if (this.subtitleMenu.classList.contains('hidden')) {
369	      this._renderSubtitleMenu();
370	      this.subtitleMenu.classList.remove('hidden');
371	    } else {
372	      this._closeSubtitleMenu();
373	    }
374	  }
375	
376	  _closeSubtitleMenu() {
377	    this.subtitleMenu.classList.add('hidden');
378	  }
379	
380	  _renderSubtitleMenu() {
381	    const tracks = Array.from(this.video.textTracks);
382	    this.subtitleTrackList.innerHTML = '';
383	
384	    const offItem = document.createElement('div');
385	    offItem.className = 'subtitle-track-item' + (tracks.every(t => t.mode === 'disabled') ? ' active' : '');
386	    offItem.textContent = 'Off';
387	    offItem.addEventListener('click', () => {
388	      disableAllTracks(this.video);
389	      setSetting('subtitlesEnabled', false);
390	      this._renderSubtitleMenu();
391	    });
392	    this.subtitleTrackList.appendChild(offItem);
393	
394	    tracks.forEach((track, i) => {
395	      const item = document.createElement('div');
396	      item.className = 'subtitle-track-item' + (track.mode === 'showing' ? ' active' : '');
397	      item.textContent = track.label || `Track ${i + 1}`;
398	      item.addEventListener('click', () => {
399	        enableTrack(this.video, i);
400	        setSetting('subtitlesEnabled', true);
401	        this._renderSubtitleMenu();
402	      });
403	      this.subtitleTrackList.appendChild(item);
404	    });
405	  }
406	
407	  async _loadExternalSub(e) {
408	    const file = e.target.files[0];
409	    if (!file) return;
410	    const text = await file.text();
411	    const ext = file.name.split('.').pop().toLowerCase();
412	    const { srtToVtt } = await import('./subtitles.js');
413	    const vttContent = ext === 'srt' ? srtToVtt(text) : text;
414	    const blob = new Blob([vttContent], { type: 'text/vtt' });
415	    const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
416	    addTrack(this.video, url, file.name);
417	    this._renderSubtitleMenu();
418	    enableTrack(this.video, Array.from(this.video.textTracks).length - 1);
419	    e.target.value = '';
420	  }
421	
422	  _onKey(e) {
423	    // Only handle keys when player view is active
424	    if (document.getElementById('player-view').classList.contains('hidden')) return;
425	    if (e.target.tagName === 'INPUT' || e.target.tagName === 'SELECT') return;
426	
427	    switch (e.code) {
428	      case 'Space':
429	        e.preventDefault();
430	        this.togglePlay();
431	        this._showOverlay();
432	        break;
433	      case 'ArrowLeft':
434	        e.preventDefault();
435	        this.seek(-10);
436	        this._showOverlay();
437	        break;
438	      case 'ArrowRight':
439	        e.preventDefault();
440	        this.seek(10);
441	        this._showOverlay();
442	        break;
443	      case 'ArrowUp':
444	        e.preventDefault();
445	        this.video.volume = Math.min(1, this.video.volume + 0.1);
446	        this.volumeSlider.value = this.video.volume;
447	        this._updateVolumeUI();
448	        this._showOverlay();
449	        break;
450	      case 'ArrowDown':
451	        e.preventDefault();
452	        this.video.volume = Math.max(0, this.video.volume - 0.1);
453	        this.volumeSlider.value = this.video.volume;
454	        this._updateVolumeUI();
455	        this._showOverlay();
456	        break;
457	      case 'KeyF':
458	        this.toggleFullscreen();
459	        break;
460	      case 'KeyM':
461	        this.toggleMute();
462	        this._showOverlay();
463	        break;
464	      case 'KeyC':
465	        this._toggleSubtitleMenu();
466	        break;
467	      case 'KeyN':
468	        this.onEnded();
469	        break;
470	      case 'KeyP':
471	        this.onPrev?.();
472	        break;
473	      case 'Escape':
474	        if (!document.fullscreenElement) this.onBack();
475	        break;
476	    }
477	  }
478	}
479	
480	function formatTime(s) {
481	  if (!isFinite(s) || s < 0) return '0:00';
482	  const h = Math.floor(s / 3600);
483	  const m = Math.floor((s % 3600) / 60);
484	  const sec = Math.floor(s % 60);
485	  if (h > 0) return `${h}:${String(m).padStart(2, '0')}:${String(sec).padStart(2, '0')}`;
486	  return `${m}:${String(sec).padStart(2, '0')}`;
487	}
488
```

```text
23	async function init() {
24	  // UI must always wire up, even if storage fails
25	  setupLibraryUI();
26	  setupPlayerUI();
27	  setupCast();
28	
29	  // Storage is best-effort (fails in private mode / restricted environments)

31	    await openDB();
32	    await loadSavedFolders();
33	    renderRecent();
34	  } catch (err) {
35	    console.warn('Storage unavailable, running without persistence:', err);
36	  }
37	
38	  // Warn if File System Access API is missing (non-Chrome browsers)
39	  if (!window.showDirectoryPicker) {
40	    document.getElementById('empty-state').querySelector('p').textContent =
41	      'File System Access API not supported. Please use Chrome 86+ on desktop.';

94	function setCastState(state) {
95	  for (const btn of document.querySelectorAll('.cast-btn')) {
96	    btn.dataset.castState = state;
97	    btn.title = state === 'unavailable'
98	      ? 'Cast unavailable (requires Chrome + Chromecast on same network)'
99	      : state === 'connected' ? 'Casting — click to stop' : 'Cast to TV';
100	  }
101	}
102	
103	function setupCast() {
104	  initCast((sessionState) => {
105	    if (sessionState === 'available') {
106	      state.castReady = true;
107	      setCastState('ready');
108	    } else if (sessionState === 'SESSION_STARTED' || sessionState === 'SESSION_RESUMED') {
109	      setCastState('connected');
110	    } else if (sessionState === 'SESSION_ENDED') {
111	      setCastState('ready');
112	    } else if (sessionState === 'unavailable') {
113	      setCastState('unavailable');
114	    }
115	  });
116	
117	  const doCast = async () => {
118	    const item = state.playlist[state.currentIndex];
119	    if (!item) return;
120	
121	    const url = state.player.getCurrentUrl();
122	    const title = item.title || item.video?.name || '';
123	
124	    if (!isLocalUrl(url)) {
125	      const ok = await castMedia(url, title);
126	      if (!ok) toast('Failed to cast. Make sure a Chromecast is on the same network.', 'error');
127	      return;
128	    }
129	
130	    // Local file — try companion server
131	    const companion = await pingCompanion();
132	    if (!companion) {
133	      toast(
134	        'Run the companion server first:\n  node server.js\n(in the StreamLocal folder)',
135	        'error'
136	      );
137	      return;
138	    }
139	
140	    const progressToast = showProgressToast('Uploading to local server…');
141	    try {
142	      const { castUrl } = await uploadForCast(
143	        item.video.handle,
144	        (pct) => progressToast.update(`Uploading… ${Math.round(pct * 100)}%`)
145	      );
146	      progressToast.dismiss();
147	      const ok = await castMedia(castUrl, title);
148	      if (!ok) toast('Cast failed. Make sure a Chromecast is on the same network.', 'error');
149	    } catch (err) {
150	      progressToast.dismiss();
151	      toast(`Upload failed: ${err.message}`, 'error');
152	    }
153	  };
154	
155	  document.getElementById('cast-btn-player').addEventListener('click', doCast);
156	  document.getElementById('cast-btn-library').addEventListener('click', doCast);
157	}

159	// ─── Folder management ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
160	
161	async function pickFolder() {
162	  if (!window.showDirectoryPicker) {
163	    toast('File System Access API not available. Use Chrome 86+ on desktop.', 'error');
164	    return;
165	  }
166	  try {
167	    const handle = await window.showDirectoryPicker({ mode: 'read' });
168	
169	    // Avoid duplicates
170	    const existing = state.folders.find(f => f.handle && f.handle.name === handle.name);
171	    if (existing) {
172	      await scanFolder(existing);
173	      return;
174	    }
175	
176	    const id = Date.now();
177	    const folder = { id, name: handle.name, handle };
178	
179	    // Store the handle itself — FileSystemDirectoryHandle is structured-cloneable
180	    try { await db_put('folders', { id, name: handle.name, handle }); } catch {}
181	
182	    state.folders.push(folder);
183	    renderFolderList();
184	    await scanFolder(folder);
185	  } catch (err) {
186	    if (err.name !== 'AbortError') {
187	      console.error('pickFolder error:', err);
188	      toast(`Could not open folder: ${err.message}`, 'error');
189	    }
190	  }
191	}
192	
193	async function loadSavedFolders() {
194	  const saved = await db_getAll('folders');
195	  for (const row of saved) {
196	    const folder = { id: row.id, name: row.name, handle: row.handle || null, needsReconnect: !row.handle };
197	    state.folders.push(folder);
198	  }
199	  renderFolderList();
200	
201	  // Auto-restore folders that still have permission (no user gesture needed)
202	  for (const folder of state.folders) {
203	    if (!folder.handle) continue;
204	    try {
205	      const perm = await folder.handle.queryPermission({ mode: 'read' });
206	      if (perm === 'granted') {
207	        folder.needsReconnect = false;
208	        renderFolderList();
209	        await scanFolder(folder);
210	      } else {
211	        // Permission needs re-prompting — mark for one-click reconnect
212	        folder.needsReconnect = true;
213	        renderFolderList();
214	      }
215	    } catch {
216	      folder.needsReconnect = true;
217	    }
218	  }
219	}
220	
221	async function reconnectFolder(folder) {
222	  try {
223	    if (folder.handle) {
224	      // Re-request permission for the stored handle (requires user gesture — the click)
225	      const result = await folder.handle.requestPermission({ mode: 'read' });
226	      if (result !== 'granted') { toast('Permission denied.', 'error'); return; }
227	    } else {
228	      // No stored handle — fall back to picker
229	      const handle = await window.showDirectoryPicker({ mode: 'read' });
230	      folder.handle = handle;
231	      folder.name = handle.name;
232	      await db_put('folders', { id: folder.id, name: folder.name, handle });
233	    }
234	    folder.needsReconnect = false;
235	    renderFolderList();
236	    await scanFolder(folder);
237	  } catch (err) {
238	    if (err.name !== 'AbortError') toast('Could not reconnect folder.', 'error');
239	  }
240	}
241	
242	async function removeFolder(folder, e) {
243	  e.stopPropagation();
244	  await db_delete('folders', folder.id);
245	  state.folders = state.folders.filter(f => f.id !== folder.id);
246	  state.videos = state.videos.filter(v => v.rootName !== folder.name);
247	  renderFolderList();
248	  filterAndRender(document.getElementById('search-input').value.trim());
249	}
250	
251	async function scanFolder(folder) {
252	  showScanning(true);
253	  try {
254	    const found = await scanDirectory(folder.handle, folder.name);
255	    state.videos = state.videos.filter(v => v.rootName !== folder.name);
256	    state.videos.push(...found);
257	    filterAndRender(document.getElementById('search-input').value.trim());
258	    toast(`Found ${found.length} video${found.length !== 1 ? 's' : ''} in "${folder.name}"`);
259	  } catch (err) {
260	    toast('Error scanning folder: ' + err.message, 'error');
261	  } finally {
262	    showScanning(false);
263	  }
264	}
265	
266	function showScanning(on) {
267	  let el = document.getElementById('scanning-indicator');
268	  if (on && !el) {
269	    el = document.createElement('div');
270	    el.id = 'scanning-indicator';
271	    el.className = 'scanning-state';
272	    el.innerHTML = '<div class="spinner"></div><p>Scanning folder…</p>';
273	    document.querySelector('.library-main')?.appendChild(el);
274	    document.getElementById('empty-state').hidden = true;
275	    document.getElementById('video-grid').hidden = true;
276	    document.getElementById('library-toolbar').hidden = true;
277	  } else if (!on && el) {
278	    el.remove();
279	  }
280	}
281	
282	// ─── Rendering ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
283	
284	function renderFolderList() {
285	  const ul = document.getElementById('folder-list');
286	  ul.innerHTML = '';
287	  for (const folder of state.folders) {
288	    const li = document.createElement('li');
289	    li.innerHTML = `
290	      <svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none"><path d="M22 19C22 20.1 21.1 21 20 21H4C2.9 21 2 20.1 2 19V5C2 3.9 2.9 3 4 3H9L11 5H20C21.1 5 22 5.9 22 7V19Z" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2"/></svg>
291	      <span>${folder.name}${folder.needsReconnect ? ' ↩' : ''}</span>
292	      <span class="folder-remove" title="Remove">✕</span>
293	    `;
294	    li.querySelector('.folder-remove').addEventListener('click', (e) => removeFolder(folder, e));
295	    if (folder.needsReconnect) {
296	      li.title = 'Click to reconnect this folder';
297	      li.addEventListener('click', () => reconnectFolder(folder));
298	    } else {
299	      li.addEventListener('click', () => scanFolder(folder));
300	    }
301	    ul.appendChild(li);
302	  }
303	}
304	
305	async function renderRecent() {
306	  const recent = await getRecentlyWatched(10);
307	  const ul = document.getElementById('recent-list');
308	  ul.innerHTML = '';
309	  for (const prog of recent) {
310	    const parts = prog.key.split('::');
311	    const name = parts[parts.length - 1]?.split('/').pop()?.replace(/\.[^.]+$/, '') || prog.key;
312	    const li = document.createElement('li');
313	    li.innerHTML = `<span>${name}</span>`;
314	    li.title = name;
315	    li.addEventListener('click', () => {
316	      const video = state.videos.find(v => v.key === prog.key);
317	      if (video) openPlayer(state.videos.indexOf(video), state.videos);
318	      else toast('Video not found — reopen its folder first.');
319	    });
320	    ul.appendChild(li);
321	  }
322	}
323	
324	function filterAndRender(query = '') {
325	  let list = [...state.videos];
326	  if (query) {
327	    const q = query.toLowerCase();
328	    list = list.filter(v => v.name.toLowerCase().includes(q) || v.relPath.toLowerCase().includes(q));
329	  }
330	  list = sortVideos(list, state.sort);
331	  state.filtered = list;
332	  renderVideoGrid(list);
333	}
334	
335	async function renderVideoGrid(videos) {
336	  const grid = document.getElementById('video-grid');
337	  const empty = document.getElementById('empty-state');
338	  const toolbar = document.getElementById('library-toolbar');
339	
340	  if (videos.length === 0) {
341	    grid.hidden = true;
342	    toolbar.hidden = true;
343	    if (state.videos.length === 0) empty.hidden = false;
344	    return;
345	  }
346	
347	  empty.hidden = true;
348	  toolbar.hidden = false;
349	  document.getElementById('video-count').textContent = `${videos.length} video${videos.length !== 1 ? 's' : ''}`;
350	
351	  grid.hidden = false;
352	  grid.className = `video-grid${state.layout === 'list' ? ' layout-list' : ''}`;
353	  grid.innerHTML = '';
354	
355	  const progressMap = {};
356	  const progs = await db_getAll('progress');
357	  for (const p of progs) progressMap[p.key] = p;
358	
359	  for (const video of videos) {
360	    const prog = progressMap[video.key];
361	    const pct = prog && prog.duration ? Math.round((prog.position / prog.duration) * 100) : 0;
362	    const card = buildCard(video, pct, prog?.completed);
363	    card.addEventListener('click', () => openPlayer(state.filtered.indexOf(video), state.filtered));
364	    grid.appendChild(card);
365	  }
366	}
367	
368	function buildCard(video, pct = 0, completed = false) {
369	  const card = document.createElement('div');
370	  card.className = 'video-card';
371	  card.dataset.key = video.key;
372	
373	  const sizeStr = formatSize(video.size);
374	  const hasSubs = video.subtitles.length > 0;
375	
376	  card.innerHTML = `
377	    <div class="card-thumb">
378	      <div class="card-thumb-placeholder">
379	        <svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none"><path d="M5 3L19 12L5 21V3Z" fill="currentColor" opacity="0.4"/></svg>
380	      </div>
381	      ${pct > 0 ? `<div class="card-progress"><div class="card-progress-fill" style="width:${pct}%"></div></div>` : ''}
382	    </div>
383	    <div class="card-info">
384	      <div class="card-title" title="${video.name}">${video.baseName}</div>
385	      <div class="card-meta">${sizeStr} · ${video.ext.toUpperCase()}</div>
386	      <div class="card-badges">
387	        ${hasSubs ? '<span class="badge badge-sub">CC</span>' : ''}
388	        ${completed ? '<span class="badge badge-watched">Watched</span>' : ''}
389	      </div>
390	    </div>
391	  `;
392	
393	  // Lazy thumbnail via IntersectionObserver
394	  const thumb = card.querySelector('.card-thumb');
395	  const observer = new IntersectionObserver((entries) => {
396	    if (entries[0].isIntersecting) {
397	      observer.disconnect();
398	      loadCardThumbnail(video, thumb);
399	    }
400	  }, { threshold: 0.1 });
401	  observer.observe(card);
402	
403	  return card;
404	}
405	
406	async function loadCardThumbnail(video, thumbEl) {
407	  try {
408	    const cached = await db_get('thumbnails', video.key);
409	    if (cached?.dataUrl) {
410	      setCardThumb(thumbEl, cached.dataUrl);
411	      return;
412	    }
413	  } catch {}
414	
415	  const dataUrl = await generateThumbnail(video.handle);
416	  if (dataUrl) {
417	    setCardThumb(thumbEl, dataUrl);
418	    try {
419	      await db_put('thumbnails', { key: video.key, dataUrl });
420	    } catch {}
421	  }
422	}
423	
424	function setCardThumb(thumbEl, dataUrl) {
425	  const placeholder = thumbEl.querySelector('.card-thumb-placeholder');
426	  if (!placeholder) return;
427	  const img = document.createElement('img');
428	  img.src = dataUrl;
429	  img.alt = '';
430	  img.loading = 'lazy';
431	  placeholder.replaceWith(img);
432	}
433	
434	// ─── Playback ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
435	
436	function openPlayer(index, videoList) {
437	  state.playlist = buildPlaylistFromVideos(videoList);
438	  state.currentIndex = index;
439	
440	  showPlayer();
441	  playAtIndex(index);
442	}
443	
444	function playAtIndex(index) {
445	  if (index < 0 || index >= state.playlist.length) return;
446	  state.currentIndex = index;
447	
448	  const entry = state.playlist[index].video;
449	  state.player.load(entry, true);
450	  renderPlaylistSidebar();
451	  renderRecent();
452	}
453	
454	function playNext() {
455	  if (state.currentIndex < state.playlist.length - 1) {
456	    playAtIndex(state.currentIndex + 1);
457	  } else {
458	    toast('End of playlist');
459	  }
460	}
461	
462	function playPrev() {
463	  if (state.player.video.currentTime > 5) {
464	    state.player.seekTo(0);
465	  } else if (state.currentIndex > 0) {
466	    playAtIndex(state.currentIndex - 1);
467	  }
468	}
469	
470	function renderPlaylistSidebar() {
471	  const ul = document.getElementById('playlist-items');
472	  ul.innerHTML = '';
473	  state.playlist.forEach((item, i) => {
474	    const li = document.createElement('li');
475	    li.className = 'playlist-item' + (i === state.currentIndex ? ' active' : '');
476	    li.innerHTML = `
477	      <span class="playlist-item-num">${i + 1}</span>
478	      <div class="playlist-item-info">
479	        <div class="playlist-item-title" title="${item.title}">${item.title}</div>
480	        <div class="playlist-item-meta">${item.video.ext.toUpperCase()} · ${formatSize(item.video.size)}</div>
481	      </div>
482	      <svg class="playlist-item-playing" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="currentColor"><path d="M5 3L19 12L5 21V3Z"/></svg>
483	    `;
484	    li.addEventListener('click', () => playAtIndex(i));
485	    ul.appendChild(li);
486	  });
487	
488	  // Scroll active item into view
489	  const active = ul.querySelector('.active');
490	  active?.scrollIntoView({ block: 'nearest' });
491	}
492	
493	// ─── M3U playlist ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
494	
495	async function onPlaylistFileSelected(e) {
496	  const file = e.target.files[0];
497	  if (!file) return;
498	  const text = await file.text();
499	  const items = parseM3U(text);
500	
501	  if (!items.length) { toast('No entries found in playlist.', 'error'); return; }
502	
503	  // Try to match items to loaded videos by filename
504	  const matched = [];
505	  for (const item of items) {
506	    const name = item.path.split(/[/\\]/).pop();
507	    const video = state.videos.find(v => v.name === name);
508	    if (video) matched.push(video);
509	  }
510	
511	  if (!matched.length) {
512	    toast(`Playlist loaded but no matching videos found. Open the folder containing the videos first.`, 'error');
513	  } else {
514	    toast(`Playlist: ${matched.length} / ${items.length} videos matched`);
515	    openPlayer(0, matched);
516	  }
517	
518	  e.target.value = '';
519	}
520	
521	// ─── View switching ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
522	
523	function showLibrary() {
524	  document.getElementById('library-view').classList.remove('hidden');
525	  document.getElementById('library-view').classList.add('active');
526	  document.getElementById('player-view').classList.add('hidden');
527	  state.player.video.pause();
528	  renderRecent();
529	  filterAndRender(document.getElementById('search-input').value.trim());
530	}
531	
532	function showPlayer() {
533	  document.getElementById('library-view').classList.add('hidden');
534	  document.getElementById('player-view').classList.remove('hidden');
535	}
536	
537	// ─── Toast ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
538	
539	function showProgressToast(msg) {
540	  const el = document.createElement('div');
541	  el.className = 'toast';
542	  el.textContent = msg;
543	  document.getElementById('toast-container').appendChild(el);
544	  return {
545	    update: (text) => { el.textContent = text; },
546	    dismiss: () => {
547	      el.style.animation = 'toast-out 0.3s ease forwards';
548	      setTimeout(() => el.remove(), 300);
549	    },
550	  };
551	}
552	
553	function toast(msg, type = '') {
554	  const el = document.createElement('div');
555	  el.className = `toast${type ? ' toast-' + type : ''}`;
556	  el.textContent = msg;
557	  document.getElementById('toast-container').appendChild(el);
558	  setTimeout(() => {
559	    el.style.animation = 'toast-out 0.3s ease forwards';
560	    setTimeout(() => el.remove(), 300);
561	  }, 3500);
562	}
563	
564	// ─── Bootstrap ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
565	
566	init().catch(console.error);
567	
568
```
